```python
total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots

c0 = counts.get("0", 0)
c1 = counts.get("1", 0)

p0 = c0 / shots
p1 = c1 / shots

# For ry(2*pi/3)|0>, expected probabilities are P(0)=cos^2(pi/3)=0.25 and P(1)=sin^2(pi/3)=0.75
assert c1 > c0
assert abs((p0 + p1) - 1.0) < 1e-9
assert 0.15 <= p0 <= 0.35
assert 0.65 <= p1 <= 0.85
```

```python
total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"Total counts {total} != shots {shots}"

c0 = counts.get("0", 0)
c1 = counts.get("1", 0)

p0 = c0 / shots
p1 = c1 / shots

expected_p0 = 0.25
expected_p1 = 0.75

assert c1 > c0, f"Expected non-uniform distribution favoring '1', got counts={counts}"
assert abs(p0 - expected_p0) < 0.15, f"Observed p('0')={p0:.3f}, expected about {expected_p0}"
assert abs(p1 - expected_p1) < 0.15, f"Observed p('1')={p1:.3f}, expected about {expected_p1}"
assert 0.55 < p1 < 0.95, f"Measured distribution not sufficiently consistent with expected non-uniform outcome: {counts}"
```

```python
expected_p0 = 0.25
```

```python
total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"Total counts {total} != shots {shots}"

n0 = counts.get("0", 0)
n1 = counts.get("1", 0)

p0 = n0 / shots
p1 = n1 / shots

# For ry(2*pi/3)|0>, ideal probabilities are P(0)=cos^2(pi/3)=0.25 and P(1)=sin^2(pi/3)=0.75
assert n1 > n0, f"Expected non-uniform distribution favoring '1', got counts={counts}"
assert abs((p0 + p1) - 1.0) < 1e-9, f"Probabilities do not sum to 1: p0={p0}, p1={p1}"
assert 0.15 <= p0 <= 0.35, f"Observed P(0)={p0} too far from expected ~0.25"
assert 0.65 <= p1 <= 0.85, f"Observed P(1)={p1} too far from expected ~0.75"
```

```python
total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots

p0 = counts.get("0", 0) / shots
p1 = counts.get("1", 0) / shots

# For ry(theta)|0>, measurement probabilities are:
# P(0) = cos^2(theta/2), P(1) = sin^2(theta/2)
# Here theta = 2*pi/3, so P(0)=0.25 and P(1)=0.75
assert counts.get("1", 0) > counts.get("0", 0)
assert abs((p0 + p1) - 1.0) < 1e-9
tol = metadata.get("tolerance", 0.15) if isinstance(metadata, dict) else 0.15
assert abs(p0 - 0.25) <= tol
assert abs(p1 - 0.75) <= tol
```

```python
total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"Counts total {total} does not match shots {shots}"

p0 = counts.get("0", 0) / shots
p1 = counts.get("1", 0) / shots

# For ry(2*pi/3)|0>, expected probabilities are P(0)=cos^2(pi/3)=0.25, P(1)=sin^2(pi/3)=0.75
assert counts.get("0", 0) > 0, "Expected some '0' outcomes"
assert counts.get("1", 0) > 0, "Expected some '1' outcomes"
assert p1 > p0, f"Expected non-uniform distribution with '1' more likely than '0', got p0={p0}, p1={p1}"
assert abs(p0 - 0.25) <= 0.15, f"Observed P(0)={p0} is too far from expected 0.25"
assert abs(p1 - 0.75) <= 0.15, f"Observed P(1)={p1} is too far from expected 0.75"
assert abs((p0 + p1) - 1.0) <= 1e-12, f"Probabilities do not sum to 1: p0+p1={p0+p1}"
```

```python
assert c1 > c0

f1 = c1 / shots
```

```python
total = sum(counts.values())

c0 = counts.get("0", 0)
c1 = counts.get("1", 0)

assert c0 > 0 and c1 > 0, f"Expected non-uniform distribution over both outcomes, got {counts}"

assert abs(p0 - 0.25) < 0.15, f"Outcome '0' frequency {p0} deviates too much from expected 0.25"
assert abs(p1 - 0.75) < 0.15, f"Outcome '1' frequency {p1} deviates too much from expected 0.75"
```
